channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Court orders Biden admin to stop selling border wall materials, was 'illegally subverting' laws: Texas AG
item.description: The Biden administration has been ordered to stop selling off border materials ahead of the incoming Trump administration next month.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden White House to send $1.25 billion in weapon aid to Ukraine before Trump transition: report
item.description: The Biden White House is set to announce an additional $1.25 billion in military assistance to Ukraine as the incoming Trump administration to take office in January.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republicans gain edge in Minnesota House after DFLer drops out over residency case
item.description: Republicans gain one-seat majority heading into 2025 session as a DFL-won seat becomes vacant. A special election will come early next year.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Requiring federal workers to return to office will be hard: Comer
item.description: U.S. Rep. James Comer believes evaluating the federal workforce's efficiency is critical to eliminating federal waste.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: An online debate over foreign workers in tech shows tensions in Trump's political coalition
item.description: An online spat between factions of Donald Trump’s supporters over immigration and the tech industry has thrown internal divisions in the president-elect’s political movement into public display.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Most Americans blame insurance profits and denials alongside the killer in UHC CEO death, poll finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Most Americans believe health insurance profits and coverage denials share responsibility for the killing of UnitedHealthcare's CEO — although not as much as the person who pulled the trigger, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: LINLEY SANDERS, TOM MURPHY and AMELIA THOMSON-DEVEAUX, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: McHenry jokes sister sent him 'the cruelest things' about him on the internet after famous gavel slam
item.description: Rep. Patrick McHenry (R-N.C.) joked that his sister sent him “the cruelest things” people said about him on the internet after his famous gavel slam affirming the ouster of former Speaker Kevin McCarthy (R-Calif.) became a meme, the News and Observer reported.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: More Americans say they need a break from political news: Survey
item.description: Most adults want a break from political news due to overload and fatigue, according to a new survey from the Associated Press-NORC Center for Public Affairs Research.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota Democratic party chair says his wins could help nationally after loss to Trump
item.description: Ken Martin helped turn around the Minnesota Democratic-Farmer-Labor Party's finances and secure a streak of statewide wins for the party over 18 years. Now, he wants to do the same thing for the Democratic National Committee.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Trump announces ambassador to Panama amid fight over canal
item.description: President-elect Trump on Wednesday announced Miami-Dade County Commissioner Kevin Marino Cabrera as his pick for U.S. ambassador to Panama. The announcement comes as Trump is sparking a feud with the U.S. ally over control of the Panama Canal.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Trump faces roadblocks to ambitious border crackdown
item.description: President-elect Trump has made ambitious promises on the border and immigration, including the biggest deportation operation ever seen. But much like in Trump’s first term in office, his immigration agenda faces significant roadblocks.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Fate of Trump's Cabinet picks unclear as Republicans prepare to take power in Senate
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The fate of President-elect Donald Trump’s Cabinet is still unclear after Republican senators spent much of December carefully dodging questions about Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump vows to direct DOJ to pursue death penalty in wake of Biden clemencies
item.description: President-elect Trump vowed Tuesday to direct his Department of Justice (DOJ) to pursue the death penalty in the wake of President Biden’s clemencies of nearly every federal death row inmate.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Biden signs $895B Defense bill despite transgender care provision
item.description: President Biden on Monday evening signed the $895 billion Defense authorization bill, despite a provision included in the massive package that restricts transgender care.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ethics releases Matt Gaetz report: 5 takeaways
item.description: The report — which spans 42 pages and includes several more in exhibits — found “substantial evidence” that Gaetz violated House rules and state and federal laws.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Biden’s death row commutations draws chorus of mixed reactions
item.description: A chorus of supporters and critics have emerged following the commutation of nearly every federal death row inmate by President Biden, whose decision came at a time when he’s trying to frame a so-far troubled legacy.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Biden didn't remove these 3 inmates from death row
item.description: Those who received the Monday commutations of their sentences saw them go from execution to life with no possibility of parole. However, three “hard cases” were not among the commutations.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Gaetz Ethics report finds ‘substantial evidence’ of statutory rape, drug use
item.description: The House Ethics Committee found “substantial evidence” that former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) paid a 17-year-old high school student for sex when he was 35, used illicit drugs like cocaine and ecstasy, and obstructed Congress’s investigation into his conduct.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Inside the Gaetz ethics report, a trove of new details alleging payments for sex and drug use
item.description: The 37-page report by the bipartisan panel includes explicit details of sex-filled parties and vacations that Matt Gaetz took part in while representing Florida's western panhandle. The findings conclude that he violated multiple state laws related to sexual misconduct while in office.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Gaetz sues Ethics Committee in last-minute bid to block release of report
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) filed a lawsuit Monday against the House Ethics Committee in a last-minute attempt to block the panel from releasing its report on him.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Broken tie in Minnesota House would give GOP muscle at start of 2025 session
item.description: Even with a slight majority, Republicans could flex newfound power at the start of the legislative session if DFL lawmaker’s disqualification stands.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Trump pushes back on 'President Musk' claims
item.description: “No, he’s not gonna be president, that I can tell you,” Trump said. “And I’m safe, you know why? He can’t be, he wasn’t born in this country.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Senate confirms 235th Biden judge, surpassing Trump’s record
item.description: The Senate confirmed 234 of Trump’s judicial nominees during his first four years in the White House.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Biden signs bill that averts a government shutdown and brings a close to days of Washington upheaval
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden signed a bill into law Saturday that averts a government shutdown, bringing a final close to days of upheaval after Congress approved a temporary funding plan just past the deadline and refused President-elect Donald Trump’s core debt demands in the package.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, FARNOUSH AMIRI and MATT BROWN, Associated Press, Fox 5
